description: OWASP ZAP is my automated security scanning staple — I run active and passive scans against web apps, integrate it into CI pipelines, and triage findings by severity before every release.
Burp Suite is my hands-on web security testing platform — intercepting traffic, manipulating requests, running Intruder attacks, and hunting vulnerabilities that automated scanners routinely miss.
Nikto gives me a rapid first-pass web server audit — scanning for outdated software, dangerous files, misconfigurations, and known vulnerabilities in minutes before deeper testing begins.

I use TheFatRat in controlled lab environments to understand payload generation and backdoor techniques — knowledge that directly sharpens how I defend real applications against similar attack vectors.
Metasploit is my exploitation framework for authorized penetration tests — I use it to validate whether discovered vulnerabilities are truly exploitable before recommending remediation priorities.
Nmap is my network reconnaissance foundation — I map open ports, fingerprint services, detect OS versions, and identify exposed attack surfaces before any security engagement begins.
Wireshark lets me see exactly what travels across the wire — I capture and dissect network packets to detect unencrypted credentials, suspicious traffic patterns, and protocol anomalies.
SQLMap automates SQL injection detection and exploitation in authorized tests — I use it to prove injection severity, extract sample data, and demonstrate real business impact to development teams.

The OWASP Top 10 is my security checklist for every project — I systematically test for injection, broken auth, IDOR, misconfiguration, and all critical risk categories before any application goes live.
Penetration testing is where I think like an attacker — scoping engagements, chaining vulnerabilities, escalating privileges, and delivering actionable reports that drive real security improvements.
I conduct structured vulnerability assessments across web, API, and infrastructure layers — cataloguing findings with CVSS scores, proof-of-concept steps, and prioritized remediation roadmaps.
I probe every input field and reflection point for XSS — testing reflected, stored, and DOM-based variants with custom payloads that bypass common filters and WAF rules.
SQL injection testing is a core part of every security audit I conduct — manually probing endpoints for error-based, blind, and time-based injection flaws that automated tools often overlook.
I test CSRF protections by crafting forged cross-origin requests — verifying that token validation, SameSite cookie flags, and origin checks are correctly enforced on every state-changing endpoint.
I run layered security scans combining SAST, DAST, and dependency checks — building automated scanning gates into CI pipelines so vulnerabilities are caught at code time, not post-deployment.
Kali Linux is my security testing operating system — its curated toolkit of offensive and defensive tools means I have everything I need for a full penetration test in a single bootable environment.
I audit SSL/TLS configurations for weak cipher suites, expired certificates, protocol downgrades, and HSTS misconfigurations — ensuring every connection is encrypted to modern security standards.
I stress-test authentication mechanisms for brute force susceptibility, account enumeration, credential stuffing vectors, and MFA bypass techniques that attackers actively exploit in the wild.
I test authorization boundaries by attempting horizontal and vertical privilege escalation — verifying that users can only access exactly what they are permitted to and nothing more.
I audit session token entropy, fixation vulnerabilities, timeout enforcement, and secure cookie attributes — ensuring sessions cannot be hijacked, forged, or reused after logout.

I validate HTTP security headers on every application I test — checking CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy configurations that form the browser-side security perimeter.
API security is a specialty of mine — I test for BOLA, mass assignment, excessive data exposure, and rate limiting gaps using the OWASP API Security Top 10 as my systematic testing guide.
